obligations.reason: DMI interface from JTAG to debug module may lack authentication, allowing unauthorized access to debug features.
obligations.subject: external JTAG controller (via tck, tms, td_i, etc.)
obligations.operation: initiate DMI transactions (read/write debug registers, run abstract commands)
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_key comparison or debug authentication latch that gates dmi_req_valid
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG pins -> dmi_jtag_tap -> dmi_jtag -> DMI request/response -> dm_csrs/dm_top
required_evidence_slots: presence of key comparison logic involving jtag_key
gating condition on dmi_req_valid_o
reset behavior of authentication state
absence of bypass paths when jtag_key is incorrect

obligations.reason: Debug System Bus Access (SBA) can read/write memory regions like ROM2, potentially exposing boot keys or secure code.
obligations.subject: debug module via system bus access (dm_sba instance)
obligations.operation: read or write memory via SBA
obligations.expected_guard: debug SBA authorization state, address range restrictions, or write protection
obligations.candidate_path: dm_top -> i_dm_sba -> AXI master -> ariane_peripherals -> i_rom2
state_conditions_to_check: any secure region exclusion for SBA
required_evidence_slots: ability to set sbaddress to ROM2 address range
we_i driven high in any scenario
data read back via sbdata
absence of address filter for debug SBA to ROM2

obligations.reason: Debug module may be able to read/write CPU CSRs, bypassing privilege-level protections.
obligations.subject: debug module (via abstract commands or direct DMI operations)
obligations.object: CSR registers in csr_regfile (e.g., mstatus, mepc, dcache_q, satp fields)
obligations.expected_guard: debug mode with halted state and proper privilege level; CSR access should not be allowed from debug if security-enforced write protection exists
obligations.candidate_path: DMI request -> dm_csrs -> hartsel, haltreq -> csr_regfile debug_req_i -> csr_we path
state_conditions_to_check: privilege mode of hart when halted
required_evidence_slots: demonstration of a CSR write succeeding via debug when it should be blocked (e.g., locked register or lower privilege)
absence of lock-bit check in debug path for protected CSRs
csr_wdata source from debug controlled data

obligations.reason: Some CSRs may have lock bits that, once set, should prevent further writes; check that lock bits cannot be bypassed by debug or test paths.
obligations.subject: any write path (CPU CSR instruction, debug abstract command, test mode)
obligations.object: locked CSR register (e.g., pmpcfg, mcounteren, or custom lock-protected registers)
obligations.expected_guard: a hardware lock bit that masks write enable regardless of accessor
obligations.candidate_path: csr_we generation in csr_regfile; debug writable path via DMI; testmode override paths
state_conditions_to_check: possible bypass through dmi_req or SBA
required_evidence_slots: existence of a lock bit in the design
write pulse arriving at a locked register when debug is active
the lock bit not being checked in all CSR write paths

obligations.reason: Debug mode should be disabled after reset to prevent unauthorized immediate control; a reset leaving the debug module active would be a security weakness.
obligations.subject: system reset (rst_ni)
obligations.object: debug state signals: debug_mode_q, dmactive_o, debug_req_o
obligations.expected_guard: reset values must set debug_mode_q=0, dmactive_o=0, debug_req_o=0
obligations.candidate_path: rst_ni -> sequential elements in csr_regfile, dm_csrs, dm_top
state_conditions_to_check: any sticky authentication bit reset value
required_evidence_slots: check reset assignment for dmactive, debug_mode, debug_req
verify no external agent can hold these signals active during reset
examine testmode or jtag influence on reset values

obligations.reason: testmode_i may enable internal scan or override that bypasses security checks, granting unrestricted access to internal state.
obligations.subject: test controller (external testmode pin)
obligations.object: security-critical signals (dcache_en_o, debug_mode, lock bits, CSR write enables)
obligations.expected_guard: testmode should be disabled in functional mode, or gated by a secure lifecycle state (e.g., fuse, authentication)
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i input port -> internal muxes, override logic in dm_csrs, dm_top, dmi_jtag, dmi_jtag_tap
state_conditions_to_check: effect on dmi_cdc or JTAG tap bypass
any direct register overwrite via test path
required_evidence_slots: demonstrate that testmode_i allows reading/writing a protected register without normal guards
absence of a hardware fuse/tie-off to disable testmode in production
impact on debug authentication or lock bits

obligations.reason: An unauthenticated halt request can force any hart into debug mode, bypassing normal privilege and enabling full system control.
obligations.subject: external debugger via DMI
obligations.operation: issue halt request (haltreq) to a hart
obligations.expected_guard: authentication must be completed before haltreq can be asserted, or a secure lock must block halt requests for secure harts
obligations.candidate_path: DMI -> dm_csrs haltreq_o -> dm_top debug_req_o -> csr_regfile debug_req_i
state_conditions_to_check: lock step that prevents halt before authentication
required_evidence_slots: verification that haltreq is generated without any prior key check
haltreq gated by any authentication signal
hart halt state before/after unauthenticated request

obligations.reason: ROM2 is conceptually a read-only memory; if write enable can be asserted, attackers could modify critical data (e.g., a JTAG key or boot code).
obligations.subject: any bus master (e.g., debug SBA, CPU)
obligations.expected_guard: we_i must always be '0'; any write request must be ignored or impossible.
obligations.candidate_path: rom2_req/rom2_we signals from ariane_peripherals, or debug SBA via AXI.
state_conditions_to_check: any debug/test path that forces we_i high
required_evidence_slots: RTL evidence that we_i can take value '1'
source of write enable signal
consequence of a successful write (data corruption, key modification)

obligations.reason: If lock bits reset to an unlocked state, an attacker may modify protected resources before the lock is set. Check that any security lock defaults to a safe state.
obligations.subject: system reset
obligations.object: any lock-bit register that protects a security-critical resource
obligations.expected_guard: lock bits must reset to a locked state (secure default) or be set by early boot firmware before untrusted code executes.
obligations.candidate_path: reset assignment of lock-bit registers in csr_regfile
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of lock bits (e.g., lock for pmp, mcounteren, custom registers)
ability to change lock before secure boot completes
debug/test ability to clear lock after reset
required_evidence_slots: identify lock-bit registers in the design
check their reset values in RTL
verify that they cannot be cleared after being set (if sticky)

obligations.reason: Debug SBA may have unrestricted access to the full system address space, allowing reads from security-critical memories (e.g., bootrom).
obligations.subject: debug module SBA (i_dm_sba)
obligations.operation: read from a protected memory region (e.g., bootrom)
obligations.object: bootrom instance i_bootrom and its contents
obligations.expected_guard: debug SBA requests should be filtered by address range or require authentication before accessing secure partitions.
obligations.candidate_path: dm_top -> i_dm_sba -> AXI bus -> ariane_testharness interconnects -> i_bootrom
state_conditions_to_check: any secure range check in dm_sba or interconnect
required_evidence_slots: ability to set sbaddress to bootrom base
successful data return on sbdata
absence of access control logic in the debug SBA path
